about.name: What Is Answer Engine Optimization (AEO)? The Complete 2026 Definition
Multi-engine coverage across ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity

mainEntity.name: What is the meaning of AEO?

acceptedAnswer.text: AEO stands for Answer Engine Optimization: the practice of making content more likely to be cited as an answer by AI engines such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.

mainEntity.name: How does AEO differ from traditional SEO?

acceptedAnswer.text: AEO is focused on being cited directly in AI answers via schema, structured definitions, and direct citations, while SEO is focused on organic search rankings using broader page-level optimization.

mainEntity.name: Is AEO the same as GEO or LLMO?

acceptedAnswer.text: The differences are minimal—the same content structures and authority signals drive visibility across AEO, GEO, and LLMO. Treating them as separate disciplines creates redundancy.
DefinedTerm, FAQPage, and Person (sameAs) schema are essential for making your content machine-readable and authoritative for AI engines.

mainEntity.name: Why do so few brands show up in AI answers?

acceptedAnswer.text: Our audit of 1,240 SaaS brands found only 11% appear as cited answers in category-level buyer prompts on ChatGPT or Perplexity, due to lack of structured data and authority signals.

mainEntity.name: Why was Notion cited in 34% of ChatGPT buyer prompts for 'knowledge management'?

acceptedAnswer.text: Notion's content uses precise definitions, supporting evidence, and authoritative schema, making it highly selected by ChatGPT for knowledge management queries.

mainEntity.name: Can brands succeed in AEO using only external citations?

acceptedAnswer.text: Some—like Splitwise—dominated AI answers through external sites like Wikipedia and Reddit. However, internal structure and authorship are still critical for long-term authority.

@graph.headline: OpenAI ChatGPT Search Documentation
@graph.text: ChatGPT Search retrieves real-time information from the web and cites sources in generated responses.

@graph.headline: Google Search Central: AI Features
@graph.text: Google AI Overviews use traditional ranking signals, schema markup, E-E-A-T authority, and content that directly answers user questions.

@graph.headline: GEO: Generative Engine Optimization (Aggarwal et al.)
@graph.text: Generative Engine Optimization is defined as a novel paradigm to aid content creators in improving their content visibility in generative engine responses.

@graph.headline: Schema.org DefinedTerm
@graph.text: A DefinedTerm is a word, name, acronym, or phrase being defined. Used with DefinedTermSet for glossaries.

@graph.headline: Gartner: AI Disruption of Search
@graph.text: By 2026, traditional search engine volume will drop 25% as AI chatbots replace conventional search for brand discovery.
